title: A Tough Decade For Real Estate Stocks
gist: Real estate stocks have badly lagged global equities over the past decade, with the MSCI real estate index trailing the MSCI World index by over 50%, as Covid-driven work-from-home trends gutted commercial property and the pandemic policy response triggered inflation and rising interest rates that undermined the low-rate thesis underpinning real estate.

insights.claim: Over the past decade the global MSCI real estate index has underperformed the MSCI World index by more than 50%.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Confirms structural underweight in listed real estate has paid off; revisiting only warranted if the rate regime reverses.
insights.quote: the global MSCI real estate index underperforming the MSCI World index by more than 50%
insights.locator: It has been a tough decade for real estate stocks

insights.claim: A decade ago money was pouring into real estate funds on the premise that interest rates would stay low for a very long time, making real estate a natural beneficiary.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: The original bull case for real estate was rate-dependent, so its viability hinges on whether low rates return.
insights.quote: money was pouring into real estate funds on the premise that interest rates would stay low for a very long time
insights.locator: In a sense, this is surprising

insights.claim: The work-from-home and shop-from-home trends that emerged during Covid crushed commercial real estate.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Structural demand impairment in offices and retail means a cyclical rate recovery may not fully rehabilitate commercial property.
insights.quote: the work-from-home/shop-from-home trend crushed commercial real estate
insights.locator: But Covid undercut the investment case

insights.claim: The fiscal and monetary policy response to Covid planted the seeds for inflation and the consequent interest rate increases that undermined real estate.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Real estate's de-rating was driven by the rate cycle, so the sector's fortunes are tied to the inflation outlook.
insights.quote: the fiscal and monetary policy response to Covid planted the seeds for inflation and the consequent interest rate increases
insights.locator: Second, the fiscal and monetary policy response
